newsSince: Since June 9 TSMC reaffirmed >30% USD revenue growth and lifted 2026 capex to ~$56B; leading-edge capacity is sold out through 2027 with demand exceeding supply ~25-30%, and 3nm price hikes of up to 15% are planned for 2H26 (plus 5-10% in 2027). Chairman Wei reiterated AI chip supply will lag demand 'for years.' This both confirms the chokepoint and resolves the prior 'no-price-hike' overhang in TSMC's favor.

sources.label: TrendForce - TSMC eyes up to 15% 3nm price hike in 2H26
SCMP - TSMC targets >30% revenue surge, ramps capex
ThePlanetTools - Wei: AI chip shortage to last for years

verdicts.rankRationale: Silicon-basket bottleneck lens: news since June 9 reinforces the manufacturing-chokepoint case - leading nodes sold out through 2027, demand exceeding supply 25-30%, and confirmed 3nm price hikes (up to 15% in 2H26) reversing the prior no-price-hike overhang and lifting margin trajectory. Rank 3 still fits; mild positive on thesis.
verdicts.bull: Sole leading-edge AI-chip bottleneck; sold out through 2027, +30% rev growth, $56B capex, now with confirmed pricing power.
verdicts.neutral: Crowded ~$2T mega-cap; thesis intact but much of the upside is consensus.
verdicts.bear: Export-rule/geopolitical overhang and a high base; AI capex cyclicality remains the tail risk.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: 3nm/2nm price hikes and AI/ASIC wafer demand into 2027
verdicts.primaryRisk: Export controls / geopolitics; AI capex digestion
